summary: Jeff Atwood announces the arrival of Coding Horror vinyl stickers and offers them free to readers who email him within a 24-hour window. The stickers are professional die-cut vinyl based on vector art provided by Steve McConnell. The giveaway quickly exceeded capacity at 350 stickers, which Atwood humorously blames on publicity from Mike Gunderloy. The offer was limited to US and Canada due to international postage complexity.
key_points: Custom two-color die-cut vinyl stickers were created from high-resolution vector art provided by Steve McConnell
The giveaway reached its 350-sticker capacity within the promotional window
International shipping complexity led to US/Canada-only availability
money_quotes: As a thank you to my readers, anyone who emails me from now until midnight PDT Wednesday, August 16th gets a free Coding Horror sticker.
Dealing with postage rate scales makes my tiny American brain hurt.
key_insight: Building reader community and engagement sometimes happens through simple gestures like giving away stickers.
